title: TOPIK I Level 1 Full Sample Exam 02

sections.title: Listening
sections.instructions: Read the transcript as the listening stimulus and choose the best answer.

questions.prompt: What is the speaker saying?

explanation.summary: 좋은 아침이에요 is a polite morning greeting, so the meaning is good morning.
teaching_points: 좋은 means good when it describes a noun.

grammar.explanation: A descriptive form can come before a noun.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 아침 with 밤, which means night.

questions.prompt: What does the woman buy?

explanation.summary: 우유하고 계란 means milk and eggs.

grammar.explanation: Connects two nouns, meaning N and N.

common_mistakes: Do not choose only one item; the woman names two things.

questions.prompt: Where is the speaker?

explanation.summary: 우체국에서 means at the post office, where the speaker sends a letter.
teaching_points: 편지를 보내요 means sends a letter.

grammar.explanation: Marks the place where an action happens.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 우체국 with 은행; 은행 is bank.

questions.prompt: What time does the meeting start?
questions.passage: Transcript: 회의는 열한 시 반에 시작해요.

explanation.summary: 열한 시 반 means eleven thirty.
teaching_points: 반 after an hour means half past.

grammar.explanation: The particle -에 marks a time when something happens.

common_mistakes: Do not drop 반; it changes eleven o'clock to eleven thirty.

questions.prompt: How is the weather?

explanation.summary: 눈이 와요 is the Korean expression for it is snowing.
teaching_points: 눈 can mean snow or eyes; with 오다 it means snow.
오늘은 sets today as the topic.

grammar.explanation: Idiomatic weather expression meaning it snows.

common_mistakes: Do not read 눈 as eyes in this weather sentence.

questions.prompt: What does the man want to drink?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 따뜻한 차를 마시고 싶어요.

explanation.summary: 따뜻한 차를 마시고 싶어요 means he wants to drink warm tea.
teaching_points: -고 싶어요 expresses wanting to do something.

grammar.explanation: Expresses a desire to do an action.

common_mistakes: Do not choose soup; 마시다 is used for drinks and the object is 차.

questions.prompt: What is Junho's job?

explanation.summary: 회사원이에요 means is an office worker or company employee.
teaching_points: 씨 is a polite title after a name.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 회사원 with 학생; 학생 means student.

questions.prompt: What day is the day after tomorrow?

explanation.summary: 모레는 월요일이에요 means the day after tomorrow is Monday.
teaching_points: 모레 means the day after tomorrow.
요일 names a day of the week.

grammar.explanation: Polite form meaning it is N.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 모레 with 내일; 내일 means tomorrow.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker do after dinner?

explanation.summary: 설거지를 해요 means washes the dishes.
common_mistakes: Do not translate 저녁 only as evening; with this context it means dinner.

questions.prompt: How much are the socks?

explanation.summary: 삼천 원 means three thousand won.
teaching_points: Sino-Korean numbers are used for prices.
common_mistakes: 삼천 is 3,000; 천 alone is 1,000.

questions.prompt: Choose the best response.

explanation.summary: 미안합니다 means I am sorry, and 괜찮아요 is a natural response meaning it is okay.
teaching_points: 괜찮아요 can reassure someone after an apology.
The other responses fit first meetings or meals.

grammar.explanation: Formal polite ending often used in set phrases.

common_mistakes: Do not answer an apology with a meal phrase like 잘 먹겠습니다.

questions.prompt: Why can't the speaker meet?
questions.passage: Transcript: 오늘은 약속이 있어서 못 만나요.

explanation.summary: 약속이 있어서 못 만나요 means because there is an appointment, the speaker cannot meet.
teaching_points: 못 before a verb means cannot.
common_mistakes: Do not infer sickness; the reason given is 약속.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker like?

explanation.summary: 딸기를 좋아해요 means I like strawberries.
teaching_points: 좋아해요 is used to say you like something.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 딸기 with 포도; 포도 means grapes.

questions.prompt: Where does the speaker live?

explanation.summary: 부산에 살아요 means I live in Busan.

grammar.explanation: Means live in or at N.

common_mistakes: With 살다, use -에 for the place, not usually -에서.

questions.prompt: What is in the drawer?

explanation.summary: 여권이 있어요 means there is a passport.
teaching_points: 서랍 안에 means inside the drawer.

grammar.explanation: Means there is N or someone has N.

common_mistakes: Do not answer drawer; the drawer is the location, not the item.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker do on weekends?

options.text: Ride a bicycle at the park

explanation.summary: 자전거를 타요 means rides a bicycle, and 공원에서 says it happens at the park.
teaching_points: 타다 is used for riding vehicles and bicycles.
common_mistakes: Do not choose bus or train just because 타다 can also mean take transportation.

questions.prompt: Who is coming tomorrow?
questions.passage: Transcript: 내일 할머니가 우리 집에 오세요.

explanation.summary: 할머니가 오세요 means grandmother is coming.
teaching_points: 오세요 is a polite form of comes.
우리 집 can naturally mean my/our house.

grammar.explanation: Uses -에 for the destination with come.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 할머니 with 할아버지; 할아버지 is grandfather.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker ask to see?

explanation.summary: 지도 좀 보여 주세요 means please show me a map.
teaching_points: 보여 주세요 asks someone to show something.

grammar.explanation: Polite request asking someone to do an action.

common_mistakes: 지도 is map; 메뉴 is menu.

questions.prompt: What does the woman order?

explanation.summary: 비빔밥하고 주스 means bibimbap and juice.
teaching_points: 주세요 makes the restaurant order polite.

grammar.explanation: A common way to order two items politely.

common_mistakes: Do not replace 주스 with water; 물 means water.

questions.prompt: What is the room number?

explanation.summary: 305호예요 means it is room 305.
teaching_points: 호 is used for room or unit numbers.

grammar.explanation: Marks a room or unit number.

common_mistakes: Read the number in order; 305 is not 503.

questions.prompt: What will the two people do?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 저녁에 같이 산책할까요? 여자: 네, 좋아요.

options.text: Take a walk together in the evening

explanation.summary: 같이 산책할까요 suggests taking a walk together, and 좋아요 accepts.
common_mistakes: 좋아요 here means agreement, not a separate description.

questions.prompt: Where will the woman go first?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 먼저 약국에 가고 그 다음에 집에 가요.

explanation.summary: 먼저 약국에 가고 means she goes to the pharmacy first.
common_mistakes: Do not choose 집; home is the second destination.

questions.prompt: What is the woman's problem?
questions.passage: Transcript: 여자: 배가 아파요. 병원에 가야 해요.

explanation.summary: 배가 아파요 means her stomach hurts.
teaching_points: 아파요 means hurts or is sick.
-아/어야 해요 means must do, here must go to the hospital.
common_mistakes: 배 can also mean pear or boat, but with 아파요 it means stomach.

questions.prompt: What does the speaker need?
questions.passage: Transcript: 오늘 햇빛이 강해요. 모자가 필요해요.

explanation.summary: 모자가 필요해요 means a hat is needed.
teaching_points: 햇빛이 강해요 gives the sunny context.
common_mistakes: Do not choose umbrella just because weather is mentioned; the item is 모자.

questions.prompt: What is the man looking for?
questions.passage: Transcript: 남자: 제 안경이 없어요. 어디 있어요?

explanation.summary: 안경이 없어요 means his glasses are missing or not here.
teaching_points: 없어요 means there is not or I do not have.
어디 있어요 asks where something is.

grammar.explanation: Means there is no N or someone does not have N.

common_mistakes: 안경 means glasses; 여권 means passport.

questions.prompt: What did the speaker do yesterday?

explanation.summary: 방을 청소했어요 means cleaned the room.
teaching_points: -했어요 marks past tense for 하다 verbs.

grammar.explanation: Polite past tense with an object.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 청소하다 with 설거지하다; 설거지하다 is wash dishes.

questions.prompt: Which season does the speaker like?

explanation.summary: 여름을 좋아해요 means I like summer.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 여름 with 겨울; 겨울 means winter.

questions.prompt: What color is the umbrella?

explanation.summary: 검은 우산 means black umbrella.
teaching_points: 검은 is the noun-modifying form of black.
제 거예요 means it is mine.

grammar.explanation: A descriptive word can come before a noun.

common_mistakes: 검은 is black; 하얀 is white.

questions.prompt: What does the man ask about?

explanation.summary: 지하철역이 어디예요 asks where the subway station is.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 지하철역 with 버스 정류장, a bus stop.

questions.prompt: What is the announcement about?
questions.passage: Transcript: 박물관은 오늘 오후 다섯 시에 닫습니다.

options.text: The bus leaves in five minutes.
The museum closes at 5 p.m. today.

explanation.summary: 오후 다섯 시에 닫습니다 means closes at 5 p.m.
teaching_points: 닫습니다 is the formal form of closes.

grammar.explanation: States when an action happens for the topic N.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 닫습니다 with 엽니다; 엽니다 means opens.

sections.title: Reading
sections.instructions: Read each sentence, notice, message, or short passage and choose the best answer.

questions.prompt: Choose the meaning of the sentence.

options.text: I eat spicy food every day.
I want to cook spicy food.

explanation.summary: 못 먹어요 means cannot eat, and 매운 음식 means spicy food.
teaching_points: 못 before a verb expresses inability.
common_mistakes: Do not ignore 못; it changes the sentence to cannot eat.

questions.prompt: Where does the person go?

explanation.summary: 병원에 가요 means goes to the hospital.
common_mistakes: 병원 is hospital; 은행 is bank.

questions.prompt: What should the person do?

explanation.summary: 창문을 열어 주세요 is a polite request to open the window.
teaching_points: -아/어 주세요 asks someone to do something.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 열다 with 닫다; 닫다 means close.

questions.prompt: Choose the best answer to the question.

explanation.summary: 어느 나라 사람이에요 asks nationality, and 베트남 사람이에요 answers Vietnamese person.
teaching_points: 사람 can identify nationality after a country name.
common_mistakes: Do not answer with time or place; the question asks country/nationality.

questions.prompt: What does the notice mean?

explanation.summary: 먹지 마세요 means do not eat.
teaching_points: -지 마세요 is a polite negative command.
common_mistakes: Do not overlook 마세요; it makes the sentence a prohibition.

questions.prompt: What is the person doing now?

explanation.summary: 편지를 써요 means writes a letter.
teaching_points: 쓰다 means write in this context.

grammar.explanation: The object marker shows what is written.

common_mistakes: 쓰다 can also mean use or be bitter, but with 편지 it means write.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the same meaning.

options.text: I want to buy a swimsuit.

explanation.summary: 가끔 means sometimes, and 수영해요 means swim.

grammar.explanation: A frequency word can come before the verb phrase.

common_mistakes: 가끔 is not 매일; 매일 means every day.

questions.prompt: What is true about the clothes?

explanation.summary: 싸지만 작아요 means cheap but small.
common_mistakes: 싸다 means cheap; 비싸다 means expensive.

questions.prompt: What is the correct order of events?

explanation.summary: 샤워하고 잠을 자요 means shower and then sleep.

grammar.explanation: Connects actions; often the first action happens before the second.

common_mistakes: Do not reverse the order; the verb before -고 appears first.

questions.prompt: What is the person asking?

explanation.summary: 얼마예요 asks the price, and 우산 means umbrella.
teaching_points: 이 means this before a noun.
common_mistakes: 얼마 asks price; 어디 asks location.

questions.prompt: What does the message ask the reader to do?

options.text: Come to the hospital tomorrow morning.

explanation.summary: 내일 오전에 병원에 오세요 asks the reader to come to the hospital tomorrow morning.
teaching_points: 오세요 is a polite request to come.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 오전 with 오후; 오후 is p.m.

questions.prompt: When is the movie?

explanation.summary: 일요일 오후 세 시 means Sunday at three in the afternoon.
teaching_points: 세 시 uses a native Korean number for the hour.
common_mistakes: Do not miss 오후; it makes the time p.m.

questions.prompt: What is true about Doyeon?

explanation.summary: 요리를 잘해요 means cooks well.
teaching_points: 잘 before a verb means well.
common_mistakes: Do not confuse 요리 with 노래; 노래 is singing/song.

questions.prompt: What does the sign mean?

options.text: Do not park a car here.
Please wait for a taxi here.

explanation.summary: 차를 세우지 마세요 means do not stop or park a car.
teaching_points: 세우다 can mean stop or park a vehicle.

grammar.explanation: Polite form meaning please do not V.

common_mistakes: Do not choose taxi; 차 here means car.

questions.prompt: What does the person want to do?

explanation.summary: 새 컴퓨터를 사고 싶어요 means I want to buy a new computer.
teaching_points: 새 before a noun means new.
-고 싶어요 expresses wanting to do something.
common_mistakes: 사다 means buy; 팔다 means sell.

questions.prompt: What is the person asking for?

explanation.summary: 차가운 물 두 병 means two bottles of cold water.
teaching_points: 두 is two before a counter.
병 is a counter for bottles.

grammar.explanation: A common way to count items.

common_mistakes: 병 counts bottles; 잔 counts cups or glasses.

questions.prompt: How does the person go to school?

explanation.summary: 지하철로 가요 means goes by subway.

grammar.explanation: Means go by N when N is a transportation method.

common_mistakes: 지하철 is subway; 버스 is bus.

questions.prompt: What is the relationship?

explanation.summary: 제 할머니 means my grandmother.
common_mistakes: 할머니 is grandmother; 할아버지 is grandfather.

questions.prompt: Why did the writer take a taxi?

explanation.summary: 비가 와서 gives the reason: because it rained.
teaching_points: 택시를 탔어요 means took a taxi.
common_mistakes: The taxi is the result, not the reason.

questions.prompt: What is being described?

explanation.summary: 크고 하얀 집 means a big white house.
teaching_points: 하얀 is the modifier form of white.
common_mistakes: 하얀 means white; 검은 means black.

questions.prompt: What is true about the pharmacy?
questions.passage: 약국은 오전 아홉 시에 열고 오후 여덟 시에 닫습니다.

options.text: It opens at 9 a.m. and closes at 8 p.m.
It opens at 8 a.m. and closes at 9 p.m.

explanation.summary: 오전 아홉 시 is 9 a.m. and 오후 여덟 시 is 8 p.m.
teaching_points: 열다 means open and 닫다 means close.
오전 and 오후 distinguish a.m. and p.m.
common_mistakes: Do not swap the opening and closing times.

questions.prompt: What should the reader bring?

explanation.summary: 여권과 사진 means passport and photo.
teaching_points: 과 connects nouns in writing/formal style.
common_mistakes: Do not choose school supplies; the text names travel/ID items.

questions.prompt: What did Jiho do?

options.text: He received a gift from a teacher.
He read a book to a friend.
He sent a text message to the teacher.

explanation.summary: 선생님께 문자를 보냈어요 means sent a text message to the teacher.
teaching_points: 보냈어요 is past tense of 보내다.

grammar.explanation: Polite marker for the recipient of an action.

common_mistakes: 보내다 means send; 받다 means receive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence best matches the dialogue?
questions.passage: 가: 숙제를 다 했어요? 나: 아니요, 아직 못 했어요.

options.text: No, I have not finished it yet.

explanation.summary: 아직 못 했어요 means have not done it yet.
teaching_points: 못 했어요 means could not/did not do.

grammar.explanation: Means have not been able to V yet.

common_mistakes: 아니요 signals a negative answer, so do not choose yes.

questions.prompt: What does the person suggest?

options.text: Eat lunch if the cafe is closed
Go hiking if the weather is good
Study because the test is hard

explanation.summary: 날씨가 좋으면 means if the weather is good, and 등산합시다 means let's hike.
common_mistakes: 좋으면 means if good; 나쁘면 would mean if bad.

questions.prompt: Why was the writer late?

options.text: Because the writer did not know the way

explanation.summary: 길을 몰라서 gives the reason: because the writer did not know the way.

grammar.explanation: Connects a cause to a result.

common_mistakes: Do not read 몰라서 as 알다; 알다 means know.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer's family?
questions.passage: 우리 가족은 세 명입니다. 부모님과 저입니다.

options.text: There are four people including an older brother.
The writer has two younger sisters.
There are three people: the parents and the writer.

explanation.summary: 세 명 means three people, and the list is parents and the writer.
teaching_points: 명 is a counter for people.

grammar.explanation: A formal way to state an amount.

common_mistakes: Remember 부모님 counts as two people, not one.

questions.prompt: What does the text say about the cafe?

options.text: It has big windows and is quiet.
It has no windows and is noisy.

explanation.summary: 창문이 크고 조용해요 says the windows are big and the cafe is quiet.
teaching_points: 크다 means big when describing 창문.
common_mistakes: 조용해요 is quiet; 시끄러워요 is noisy.

questions.prompt: What did the writer buy?

explanation.summary: 감자 네 개 means four potatoes, and 샀어요 means bought.
teaching_points: 네 is four before a counter.

grammar.explanation: A common counting pattern after a noun.

common_mistakes: 감자 is potato; 귤 is tangerine.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer?
questions.passage: 저는 일본어를 배우지만 아직 잘 못해요.

options.text: The writer studies Japanese but is not good at it yet.
The writer does not want to learn Japanese.

explanation.summary: 배우지만 means learns but, and 아직 잘 못해요 means is not good at it yet.
common_mistakes: 잘 못해요 is not the same as 잘해요; 못 makes it negative.

questions.prompt: What is the best title for the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 저녁마다 집에서 요리해요. 오늘은 김치찌개를 만들었어요. 가족하고 맛있게 먹었어요.

explanation.summary: The passage is about cooking dinner at home and eating with family.
common_mistakes: Choose the title from the whole passage, not only one food word.

questions.prompt: What does the writer say about the bag?

explanation.summary: 새롭지만 비싸요 means new but expensive.
common_mistakes: 비싸요 means expensive; 싸요 means cheap.

questions.prompt: Where is the bank?
questions.passage: 은행은 지하 일 층에 있습니다. 계단 앞입니다.

options.text: On basement level 1, in front of the stairs
On the second floor next to the elevator

explanation.summary: 지하 일 층 means basement level one, and 계단 앞 means in front of the stairs.

grammar.explanation: Means it is in front of N.

common_mistakes: Do not confuse 앞 with 옆; 옆 means beside.

questions.prompt: Why does the writer like spring?
questions.passage: 저는 봄을 좋아해요. 꽃이 많이 피어서 예뻐요.

options.text: Because many flowers bloom and it is pretty

explanation.summary: 꽃이 많이 피어서 예뻐요 explains that many flowers bloom, so it is pretty.
common_mistakes: Do not choose skiing; that fits winter, not this spring passage.

questions.prompt: What will the person do first?
questions.passage: 먼저 빨래를 하고 시장에 갈 거예요.

explanation.summary: 먼저 빨래를 하고 means first do laundry, then go to the market.
common_mistakes: Do not choose the second action; 먼저 marks laundry as first.

questions.prompt: What does the invitation say?

options.text: Let's play basketball together on Friday.

explanation.summary: 같이 농구해요 invites someone to play basketball together on Friday.

grammar.explanation: Can sound like an invitation to do something together.

common_mistakes: 농구 is basketball; 축구 is soccer.

questions.prompt: What is true about the writer's friend?
questions.passage: 제 친구는 머리가 짧고 안경을 써요.

options.text: The friend has long hair and no glasses.
The friend has short hair and wears glasses.
The friend is tall and has big eyes.
The friend is busy and tired.

explanation.summary: 머리가 짧고 안경을 써요 describes short hair and wearing glasses.
teaching_points: 머리가 짧다 means hair is short.
common_mistakes: 쓰다 has several meanings; with glasses it means wear.

questions.prompt: What is the best title for the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 화요일마다 컴퓨터 수업에 가요. 한 시간 동안 타자를 연습해요. 수업이 쉽고 재미있어요.

explanation.summary: The passage describes going to computer class every Tuesday and practicing typing.
teaching_points: 한 시간 동안 means for one hour.

grammar.explanation: Means for a duration of time.

common_mistakes: Do not choose a travel title; the passage focuses on class.

questions.prompt: What can you infer from the note?
questions.passage: 민수야, 나는 운동장에 있어. 네 시에 교실에 갈게.

options.text: The writer is in the classroom and will go to the playground at four.
The writer lost a sports bag.
The writer is at the playground and will go to the classroom at four.

explanation.summary: 운동장에 있어 says the writer is at the playground, and 네 시에 교실에 갈게 says they will go to the classroom at four.
teaching_points: -ㄹ게 can state the speaker's intention informally.

grammar.explanation: States the speaker's intention or promise; 갈게 is informal/plain casual style.

common_mistakes: Do not reverse the current location and future destination.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
questions.passage: 저는 작은 서점에서 일합니다. 아침에는 책을 정리하고 오후에는 손님을 돕습니다. 조용하지만 재미있는 일입니다.

options.text: The writer buys bread every morning.
The writer wants to close a noisy cafe.
The writer works at a small bookstore and finds the quiet work interesting.
The writer studies at a big school and dislikes books.

explanation.summary: The passage describes working in a small bookstore, arranging books, helping customers, and finding the work quiet but interesting.
common_mistakes: Do not choose bakery or cafe details; this passage is about a bookstore.
